title: umbra-py render manifest
description: The JSON object umbra-py prints to stdout when a render command (change, timescan, swipe, gallery, map) is run with --json. It records exactly what artifact was produced, from which acquisitions, and with which settings -- the success-side counterpart to the machine-readable error contract. Human progress and warnings go to stderr, so stdout carries this object alone. Stable within a minor version.

output.description: Filesystem path of the artifact that was written (the image, GIF, or HTML page).

items_used.description: The ids of the acquisitions the artifact was built from, in render order.

parameters.description: The render settings the command used. The exact keys depend on the command (e.g. 'asset', 'max_size', 'db', 'percentile', 'frames', 'mode', 'format'); a caller should read the keys present rather than assume a fixed set.

sidecars.description: Any auxiliary files the command also wrote, keyed by name. Present only when a command produces one (e.g. 'umbra change --narrate' writes a 'narration' JSON sidecar).
